name: link-two-tasks
goal: Create a dependency link from Discovery to Interviews.

steps.note: Hover the first (source) task and look for link ports on its left/right ends that would let me start a connection (SR-IXC-13).
With the task selected, are visible left/right link ports shown on the bar ends (SR-IXC-13)? A first-time user expects a dedicated port to drag from.

Naive attempt: drag from the right end of task 1 onto task 2 to connect them. If the right end is only a resize handle, this will resize dates instead of linking, documenting the missing link port (SR-IXC-13).
Did a dependency arrow appear from the drag, or did the gesture resize the bar / do nothing (finding on drag-to-link discoverability)?
Re-select the source task (the previous drag may have changed the date or selection).
Fall back to the Link button in the app bar, the documented path to start a dependency.
Is link mode armed with a clear, discoverable prompt telling me to pick the target task (SR-IXC-13)?
Click the second task to complete the link.
Was the finish-to-start dependency created between Discovery and Interviews?
Esc should reliably cancel link mode / deselect as an escape hatch (SR-IXC-09). If link mode was already committed, Esc should at least return to the default context.
Confirm link mode has exited and the surface is neutral (no sticky link cursor or half-started connection).
